message:
fix(antipatterns): use Unicode-safe string indexing in extract_annotation

The previous implementation used byte positions from a lowercased string
to index into the original string, which would panic or produce incorrect
results when non-ASCII characters appeared before the marker.

The fix:
- Uses character counting to find the correct position in the original
  string after case-insensitive matching on the lowercased version
- Maps lowercase byte positions to character offsets, then back to
  original string byte positions using char_indices()
- Handles emoji, CJK characters, and other multi-byte Unicode correctly

Added comprehensive tests covering:
- Basic ASCII case matching
- Case-insensitive matching (ANTI-PATTERN vs anti-pattern)
- Unicode characters before marker (Japanese text, emoji)
- Empty/whitespace-only annotation handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
